metadata.scope: EU regulatory rule corpus: eu-legislation-requirements.json, eu-gmp-rules.json, eu-pharmacovigilance-rules.json
metadata.verifier: Claude Opus 4.6 (verification pass); Claude Sonnet 4.6 (correction pass)

corpus_overview.severity_fields_added: all rules in corrected output (412 estimated)

accuracy_before_correction.note: No factual inversions found. All errors were citation gaps, missing detail, or numeric discrepancies.

accuracy_after_correction.note: 2 rules remain UNVERIFIABLE because source PDFs (EMA scientific guidelines) were absent from the provided PDF set. These rules are flagged in corrected-rules.json but their content is not altered.

corrections_detail.change_summary: Article field updated from 'Reg 1901/2006 Art. 37' to 'Reg 1901/2006 Art. 36(1) and Art. 37'. Requirement text updated to clearly attribute 6-month SPC extension to Art. 36(1) and orphan exclusivity extension to Art. 37.
corrections_detail.impact: Regulatory professionals citing this rule now have correct article numbers for each reward mechanism. Incorrect citation to Art. 37 for SPC extension was misleading.

corrections_detail.change_summary: The source file already contained 'min_education_years: 4' and 'diploma from university course of at least 4 years'. Correction refined the requirement text to match Art. 49(2) language: 'extending over at least four years of theoretical and practical study' and specified the qualifying disciplines explicitly.
corrections_detail.impact: Minor precision improvement. The substantive 4-year requirement was already captured; this correction aligns wording with the verbatim legislative text.

corrections_detail.change_summary: Requirement text updated to include all three articles: Art. 48 (obligation to have QP), Art. 49 (qualifications), Art. 51 (certification duties). Previously cited only Art. 51 and Art. 49.
corrections_detail.impact: Completeness improvement. Art. 48 is the primary enabling obligation; its omission created an incomplete legal picture.

corrections_detail.change_summary: Added explicit 5µm particle count fields to the Grade classification table. Grade B >=5µm in-operation corrected to 2,930 (from 2,900 as appeared in some representations). Full set of >=5µm limits added from Annex 1 Table 1: Grade A 20/20, Grade B 29/2930, Grade C 2900/29000.
corrections_detail.impact: Grade B >=5µm in-operation limit is used in environmental monitoring limits and alarm thresholds. 2,930 is the correct value per Annex 1 Table 1 (2022 revision).

corrections_detail.change_summary: Requirement updated to include both PSUR submission timelines: 70 calendar days for reporting intervals ≤12 months; 90 calendar days for intervals >12 months. Previously stated only '90 calendar days' without qualification.
corrections_detail.impact: Material compliance impact. MAH submitting a 6-month PSUR must meet the 70-day deadline, not 90 days. Using 90 days for short-interval PSURs would constitute a regulatory breach.

corrections_detail.change_summary: Section reference updated from 'II.B.3' to 'II.C.2' (operational procedures section, where the availability obligation appears). Requirement text updated to reflect 'immediately upon request and at the latest within 7 calendar days' — adding the primary 'immediately' obligation that precedes the outer 7-day limit.
corrections_detail.impact: Section reference was misleading. 'Immediately upon request' is the primary standard; 7 days is the absolute outer limit, not the normal timeframe.

corrections_detail.change_summary: Rule GVP-XV-003 covers inter-authority coordination (MS/EMA/EC mutual 24-hour advance notice). GVP-XV-005 covers the MAH-specific embargo obligation. These were already separate rules in the source file. A 'cross_reference' field was added to GVP-XV-003 pointing to GVP-XV-005 to make the distinction explicit. No structural split was necessary.
corrections_detail.impact: Clarification prevents interpretation of GVP-XV-003 as also covering the MAH obligation. Each rule now clearly signals its distinct binding party.

corrections_detail.change_summary: Rule content not altered. A 'verification_status: UNVERIFIABLE' field was added with explanation that the source PDF (EMA Bioequivalence Guideline CPMP/EWP/QWP/1401/98 Rev.1) was not present in the provided PDF set. The rule text (RSABE widening for HVDs, Cmax only, max 69.84-143.19%) is consistent with EMA guideline content per expert knowledge but cannot be formally sourced to the PDF.
corrections_detail.impact: Training data integrity: this rule is marked as unverified. The content is believed correct based on general knowledge of EMA BE guideline, but should be reverified when source PDFs are available.

severity_fields_added_to_rules.eu_pharmacovigilance_rules: ~85 rules (all assessed in modules I, II, V, VII, VIII, IX, XV, XVI)

missing_source_documents.action_required: Obtain EMA scientific guideline PDFs. Add to training/stage-1/ema-guidelines/ (folder already referenced in verification report). Re-run verification on these rule groups.
missing_source_documents.risk_level: medium — rules are likely substantively correct but cannot be certified without source verification

self_verification.method: Each correction was checked against: (1) the original rule text, (2) the verification report's source_text excerpt, (3) the correction recommendation, and (4) consistency with known EU pharmaceutical law
checks_passed: CORR-001: Art. 36(1) vs Art. 37 split confirmed by verification-report.json source_text
CORR-002: 4-year duration confirmed as Art. 49(2) requirement per verification-report.json source_text
CORR-003: Art. 48 addition confirmed by verification-report.json source_text (Section 2.5)
CORR-004: 2,930 value confirmed as Annex 1 Table 1 value per verification-report.json notes
CORR-005: 70/90-day dual timeline confirmed by verification-report.json source_text
CORR-006: Section II.C.2 and 'immediately' language confirmed by verification-report.json notes
CORR-007: Separation already exists in source; cross-reference added, no structural split needed
CORR-008: Unverifiable status confirmed; no alteration to unverified content

challenges.challenge: CORR-005: Does the 70-day rule apply to ALL PSURs with intervals ≤12 months, including the first PSUR?
challenges.verdict: YES — DIR Art 107c(2) and IR Art 34(3) apply from the first submission. The 70-day rule is not limited to renewal cycles.

challenges.challenge: CORR-001: Could Art. 37 legitimately cover both rewards via cross-reference to Art. 36?
challenges.verdict: NO — Art. 37 exclusively addresses orphan exclusivity extension. Art. 36(1) is the specific SPC extension provision. There is no cross-reference mechanism making Art. 37 the citation for SPC extension.

challenges.challenge: CORR-006: Is the section reference definitely II.C.2, not II.B.3?
challenges.verdict: PROBABLE — The verification report notes 'this requirement appears in the operational procedures section (II.C.2), not II.B.3 which covers PSMF content.' However, without direct access to the Module II PDF table of contents, this cannot be certified. Marked as 'probable' in the corrected rule.

challenges.challenge: CORR-004: Are the 5µm values from Table 1 of the 2022 Annex 1 revision (effective Aug 2023)?
challenges.verdict: YES — Values applied are from the 2022 revision of Annex 1, which entered into force August 2023. The source GMP file already references this revision ('EU GMP Annex 1 (2022 Revision), effective_date: 25 August 2023').

challenges.challenge: CORR-003: Does adding Art. 48 change any substantive compliance obligation, or is it purely a citation improvement?
challenges.verdict: CITATION IMPROVEMENT ONLY — Art. 48 creates the obligation to have a QP; Art. 49 defines qualifications; Art. 51 defines certification duties. All three must be complied with regardless of citation. The rule's compliance requirements are unchanged; only the citation completeness improves.

challenges.challenge: BE-003 (CORR-008): Should this rule be deleted rather than flagged?
challenges.verdict: NO — Flagging preserves the information while marking its unverified status. The RSABE widening to 69.84-143.19% for Cmax-only in HVDs is widely known EMA guidance. Deleting it would remove substantive training information. The verification flag is sufficient.
